title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns, Triggering Leadership Change
subtitle: After two years in office, the Labour leader steps down as the country prepares for its seventh prime minister in a decade.

summary.en: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as leader of the Labour Party. He will serve as a caretaker leader until a successor is selected in late July. His decision follows declining approval ratings and internal pressure from his own party. The move has led to the postponement of an important EU-UK summit. Meanwhile, international leaders have shared mixed reactions to his departure.
summary.zh: 英國首相施凱爾已宣布辭去工黨黨魁職務。他將擔任看守領導人，直到七月下旬選出接班人為止。這項決定是在支持率下滑及黨內壓力下所做出的。此舉已導致一場重要的歐盟與英國高峰會延期。與此同時，國際領導人對他的離職反應不一。

paragraphs.en: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has officially resigned as the leader of the United Kingdom’s governing Labour Party. This decision marks the end of his two-year term and sets the stage for the country to appoint its seventh prime minister in just ten years. Starmer announced that he will remain in his position as a caretaker — a temporary leader who manages daily government tasks — until his party chooses a new leader. He has asked the Labour Party to begin the process of finding his replacement on July 9.
paragraphs.zh: 英國首相施凱爾（Keir Starmer）已正式辭去執政黨工黨（Labour Party）黨魁職務。這項決定為他兩年的任期畫下句點，也讓英國即將在短短十年內迎來第七位首相。施凱爾宣布，在黨內選出新任領導人之前，他將以看守身分留任，負責處理政府日常事務。他已要求工黨於七月九日啟動尋找接班人的程序。

paragraphs.en: In a tearful speech outside 10 Downing Street, Starmer addressed the reasons behind his departure. He explained that he had listened to members of his own party who questioned if he was the right person to lead them into the next general election. "I have heard the answer of my parliamentary party to that question, and I accept the answer with good grace," Starmer said. While he admitted that his time in office had been difficult, he defended his record, pointing to increased spending on healthcare and defense, as well as a decrease in undocumented migration.
paragraphs.zh: 在唐寧街十號外發表的一場感性演說中，施凱爾說明了他離職的原因。他解釋說，他聽取了黨內成員的意見，這些成員質疑他是否仍是帶領工黨投入下屆大選的合適人選。施凱爾表示：「我聽到了國會黨團對此問題的回答，我大方地接受這個結果。」儘管他承認任內充滿挑戰，但他仍為自己的政績辯護，並指出任內增加了醫療保健與國防支出，同時也減少了非法移民的數量。

paragraphs.en: Starmer’s resignation comes after a period of significant political pressure. According to NPR News, his personal approval ratings had fallen to a historic low. Many voters felt that he failed to deliver the real change they expected after 14 years of Conservative Party rule. Additionally, the rise of a new anti-immigration party, Reform UK, caused concern among Labour lawmakers. As the party’s popularity grew in local elections, many in the Labour Party began calling for Starmer to step down to avoid further losses.
paragraphs.zh: 施凱爾的辭職是在經歷了一段嚴重的政治壓力後發生的。根據美國全國公共廣播電台（NPR）報導，他的個人支持率已跌至歷史新低。許多選民認為，在保守黨（Conservative Party）執政十四年後，他未能兌現大眾所期待的真正變革。此外，新興的反移民政黨「英國改革黨」（Reform UK）的崛起，也讓工黨議員感到擔憂。隨著該黨在地方選舉中的聲勢看漲，工黨內部許多人開始要求施凱爾下台，以避免進一步的選票流失。

paragraphs.en: It appears that the transition of power may happen quickly. Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester, has already confirmed his intention to run for the leadership. With support from other prominent figures like former Health Secretary Wes Streeting, it is possible that Burnham could become the next prime minister without facing a contest. If this happens, a new leader could enter office by late July.
paragraphs.zh: 權力交接似乎會迅速完成。大曼徹斯特（Greater Manchester）前市長伯納姆（Andy Burnham）已證實有意角逐黨魁。在衛生大臣魏斯特（Wes Streeting）等其他重要人物的支持下，伯納姆極有可能在無人競爭的情況下成為下一任首相。若情況如此，新領導人最快可能在七月下旬就職。

paragraphs.en: The news of Starmer’s resignation has had an immediate effect on international relations. The Guardian reports that a planned EU-UK summit, which was meant to take place on July 22, has been postponed. European Council President António Costa stated that the meeting must be delayed while the European Union waits to see who will take over. However, many leaders expressed their appreciation for Starmer’s work. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen thanked him, noting that European and Ukrainian security is stronger because of his leadership. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy also offered warm praise, calling Starmer a welcome guest in Ukraine.
paragraphs.zh: 施凱爾辭職的消息對國際關係產生了立即性的影響。《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，原定於七月二十二日舉行的歐盟與英國高峰會已經延期。歐洲理事會主席柯斯塔（António Costa）表示，在歐盟等待確認接任人選期間，會議必須推遲。不過，許多領導人對施凱爾的工作表示肯定。歐盟執委會主席范德賴恩（Ursula von der Leyen）感謝他的貢獻，並指出歐洲與烏克蘭的安全因他的領導而更加穩固。烏克蘭總統澤倫斯基（Volodymyr Zelenskyy）也給予高度評價，稱施凱爾是烏克蘭受歡迎的貴賓。

paragraphs.en: Not all international reactions were positive. Donald Trump, who has had a complicated relationship with the British government, criticized Starmer’s performance on immigration and energy policy before the resignation was even announced. Trump has previously expressed frustration with the UK over various issues, including military support and trade. Meanwhile, the Kremlin’s spokesperson remarked that Starmer had not done anything special to change the relationship between the UK and Russia, suggesting that a new leader would likely not change that position either.
paragraphs.zh: 並非所有國際反應都是正面的。與英國政府關係複雜的川普（Donald Trump），在辭職消息宣布前就曾批評施凱爾在移民與能源政策上的表現。川普先前曾因軍事援助與貿易等各種議題，對英國表達不滿。與此同時，克里姆林宮發言人則表示，施凱爾在改變英俄關係方面並無特殊作為，並暗示新領導人恐怕也不會改變此一立場。

paragraphs.en: As the UK prepares for this change, the country is also marking a significant anniversary. Tuesday is the 10th anniversary of the Brexit vote, the decision for Britain to leave the European Union. Experts like John McTernan, a former political secretary to Prime Minister Tony Blair, suggest that the economic struggles following Brexit have contributed to the public's anger toward the governing class. This frustration has created a difficult environment for any leader, as the nation continues to deal with the long-term effects of economic changes and political uncertainty.
paragraphs.zh: 在英國準備迎接變革之際，該國也正值一個重要的週年紀念。週二是英國脫歐（Brexit）公投十週年，即英國決定脫離歐盟的日子。曾任首相布萊爾（Tony Blair）政治秘書的麥特南（John McTernan）等專家認為，脫歐後的經濟困境加劇了民眾對執政階層的憤怒。這種挫折感為任何領導人創造了艱難的環境，因為國家仍需應對經濟變遷與政治不確定性所帶來的長期影響。

paragraphs.en: Despite the challenges, Starmer promised to give his successor his full support. He expressed hope that the next leader will inherit a country that is stronger and fairer. For now, the focus of British politics shifts to the upcoming leadership selection, as the nation waits to see who will take the lead during this period of transition. While the political future remains uncertain, the international community continues to watch closely, hoping for stability in the UK’s foreign policy and its ongoing partnerships.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨挑戰，施凱爾仍承諾會全力支持繼任者。他表示希望下一任領導人能接手一個更強大、更公平的國家。目前，英國政治焦點已轉向即將到來的黨魁選舉，全國都在關注誰將在過渡時期接掌大權。雖然政治前景仍不明朗，但國際社會持續密切關注，期盼英國的外交政策與既有夥伴關係能保持穩定。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To prepare the conditions for something to happen.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上是「設置舞台」，這裡引申為創造條件。文中：This decision marks the end of his two-year term and sets the stage for the country to appoint its seventh prime minister in just ten years.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To resign from a position or job.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於政治新聞，指主動放棄職位。文中：As the party’s popularity grew in local elections, many in the Labour Party began calling for Starmer to step down to avoid further losses.
常見作「跑步」，這裡指參與競選。文中：Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester, has already confirmed his intention to run for the leadership.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be in the position of control or to be the first to do something.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 帶頭；領導；取得領先地位。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指在過渡時期承擔領導責任。文中：For now, the focus of British politics shifts to the upcoming leadership selection, as the nation waits to see who will take the lead during this period of transition.

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: Keir Starmer has resigned, paving way for a 7th U.K. prime minister in 10 years

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: European leaders pay tribute to Starmer as EU-UK summit is postponed

previousArticles.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns, Setting Stage for New Leadership
previousArticles.summaryEn: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as leader of the Labour Party. He will remain in his role as a caretaker until a successor is chosen in late July. His departure follows low approval ratings and pressure from within his own party. Former mayor Andy Burnham is currently the leading candidate to take his place. International leaders have responded to the news with a mix of praise and political commentary.

quiz.question: What specific action did Keir Starmer take regarding the Labour Party's leadership selection process?

options.A: He immediately appointed Andy Burnham as his successor.
options.B: He requested that the party begin the search for a new leader on July 9.
options.C: He insisted on a competitive election process to ensure a fair transition.
options.D: He decided to remain in office until the end of the year to oversee the selection.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段最後一句：「He has asked the Labour Party to begin the process of finding his replacement on July 9.」，明確指出他要求黨內於 7 月 9 日開始尋找接班人的流程。選項 A 錯誤，因為 Burnham 只是有意參選，並非由 Starmer 直接任命；選項 C 錯誤，文中提到 Burnham 可能不需經過競選（without facing a contest）；選項 D 錯誤，文中提到他會擔任看守領袖直到新領袖選出，並非直到年底。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why might the Labour Party have felt pressured to replace Starmer?

options.A: Because Starmer refused to cooperate with the European Union on security matters.
options.B: Because the party feared losing more support due to the rise of Reform UK.
options.C: Because Starmer was personally responsible for the economic struggles following Brexit.
options.D: Because the Labour Party had already decided to merge with the Conservative Party.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，Starmer 的支持率跌至歷史新低，且文中提到：「the rise of a new anti-immigration party, Reform UK, caused concern among Labour lawmakers. As the party’s popularity grew... many in the Labour Party began calling for Starmer to step down to avoid further losses.」，這顯示黨內是為了應對政治競爭對手的威脅而要求他下台。選項 A 與文意不符，因為歐盟領袖反而感謝他的貢獻；選項 C 雖然提到 Brexit 造成經濟困難，但並未將責任歸咎於 Starmer 個人；選項 D 為無中生有。

quiz.question: In the second paragraph, what does the phrase 'accept the answer with good grace' mean?

options.A: To accept a difficult situation in a polite and calm manner.
options.B: To reject the outcome because it was based on unfair criticism.
options.C: To express deep anger while still following the rules.
options.D: To show that the person is very happy with the final decision.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。片語 'with good grace' 在此語境下指「欣然地、有風度地」接受。Starmer 雖然被黨員質疑是否適合領導，但他選擇以體面的方式接受這個結果，而非憤怒反擊或拒絕。選項 B 與文意衝突；選項 C 的 anger 與 'good grace' 的積極禮貌態度相反；選項 D 錯誤，因為這是一個艱難的辭職決定，Starmer 並非感到快樂。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of this article?

options.A: A detailed analysis of why the Brexit vote occurred ten years ago.
options.B: A critique of Donald Trump's influence on British energy policy.
options.C: A report on the resignation of Keir Starmer and the resulting political transition.
options.D: A biography of Andy Burnham's career as the mayor of Greater Manchester.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心圍繞在英國首相 Keir Starmer 的辭職事件，包括他辭職的原因、黨內的反應、國際社會的看法，以及接下來英國政治過渡期的不確定性。選項 A 僅是背景資訊之一；選項 B 僅是文中提到的一個小插曲；選項 D 僅提到 Burnham 是潛在繼任者之一，並非文章主旨。因此 C 是最能概括全文重點的選項。
